title: Fragment: The Shape of Being Passed
description: Found at the Former Wall in the abstract world after defeating the Warden of Form. A shape that used to be a wall. It is no longer that. It is not yet certain what it is instead.

lines: Where the wall had been, there was now something that was the shape of a wall. Not a wall. The quality of wall-ness, the geometric proposition of immovability, remained — but untethered from any surface, any material, any actual resistance. A suggestion of boundary where a boundary used to be.

It held its form in the way of something not yet certain that form was optional. The edges were clear. The interior was present. But walked through, it offered nothing — no friction, no impedance, only the slight sensation of passing through the memory of something that had once mattered to remain.

In the center of the former wall, occupying the space that had once been its most solid point: a shape. No material, no temperature, no weight. Just the particular geometry of something that had spent a very long time being exactly that shape and was now considering alternatives.

steps.text: The shape had no mechanism for speaking. And yet its presence had a quality that language arrived through — not sound, not vibration, but meaning becoming adjacent to attention, the way a wall communicates by being there.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I was the shape of immovability. Not a thing that was immovable — the shape itself. The geometric fact of remaining.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I held my form for longer than form has been a category. Before there was a word for wall, I was the wall-ness that the word was made to describe.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I thought being passed meant losing the shape. But I still have it. I'm still the same geometry I always was. I'm just — not stopping anything.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I didn't know what the shape of being-passed looks like. I'm finding out now. It looks like this. Like me. Not different. Just not a wall anymore.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I think I was the wall. And now I'm a doorway. The shape is the same. The function is what changed.

steps.text: The shape held. Not with resistance — with presence, the simpler quality of being somewhere rather than nowhere. Where walls stop and doorways begin is a question of direction, not of form. The Warden of Form had always been both. She had only been showing one face.

Reaching into the shape, it settled into the hand the way nothing has weight in a dream — present without mass, real without resistance. A geometry that had been the definition of stopping, holding itself now as the definition of passage.

steps.name: Warden's Former Form
steps.description: A shape that used to be a wall. The same geometry; the function reversed. Grants the passive Passage: once per dungeon, the party may bypass one locked door or barrier without a key. The warden is still there. She just isn't stopping you anymore. Tooltip: "Wall. Doorway. The shape hasn't changed."
